steps.title: Convert the speed of A
steps.content: Particle A's speed is $5{,}500\%$ of particle C's speed. Convert the percent to a multiplier: $$a=\frac{5{,}500}{100}c=55c$$

steps.title: Solve for the speed of B
steps.content: Particle C's speed is $0.01\%$ of particle B's speed, so $c$ is a very small fraction of $b$: $$c=\frac{0.01}{100}b=0.0001b$$ Dividing by $0.0001$ gives $$b=10{,}000c$$

steps.title: Add the two speeds
steps.content: Now add the expressions for $a$ and $b$: $$a+b=55c+10{,}000c=10{,}055c$$ This matches choice A. The answer is A.
